title: How agents are transforming work
author: OpenAI
why_it_matters: OpenAI uses Codex usage data to show the shift from chatbot interactions to delegated long-horizon work.
actionable_takeaway: Turn high-signal items into delegated Codex tasks with evidence, validation, and a reusable output format.

title: ChatGPT for iOS 1.2026.181 adds Codex task management
author: OpenAI Developers
why_it_matters: The July 6 Codex changelog says iOS can create, search, open, fork, and manage Codex tasks from a conversation.
actionable_takeaway: Use iPhone as the capture and task-management surface, then review outputs on Mac.

title: Mastering Codex Remote for engineering
author: Thomas Ricouard / OpenAI Developers
why_it_matters: Codex Remote is framed as a phone control center for starting, steering, reviewing, and organizing work.
actionable_takeaway: Create a repeatable mobile flow: start task on phone, let Codex run, finish review on Mac.

title: Making private MCP servers reachable without making them public
author: Denys Kurylenko / OpenAI Developers
why_it_matters: Private tools can be connected without making local servers public, which matches the user's local bridge safety boundary.
actionable_takeaway: Keep MCP and local bridge access private-by-default; require explicit approval before any public tunnel.

title: Introducing Codex
author: OpenAI
why_it_matters: The launch post defines Codex as parallel cloud tasks with verifiable terminal logs, tests, and review evidence.
actionable_takeaway: Require every delegated AI Radar task to return source links, verification notes, and failure status.

title: Codex Remote GA and DigitalOcean plugin
author: OpenAI
why_it_matters: Release notes remain the baseline for phone remote control and cloud workspace capability boundaries.
actionable_takeaway: Keep mobile Remote and cloud workspace as two separate patterns: phone controls, cloud runs heavy work.

title: OpenAI models GPT-5.5 and GPT-5.4-and Codex-now on Amazon Bedrock
author: Amazon Staff
why_it_matters: Codex is being distributed through enterprise cloud controls such as IAM, VPC isolation, encryption, and CloudTrail.
actionable_takeaway: For company workflows, evaluate Codex by identity, logs, data boundary, and procurement path, not only model quality.

title: AWS Summit New York 2026: New ways to make AI agents more effective at work
author: Swami Sivasubramanian / AWS
why_it_matters: AWS emphasizes context graphs, mobile orchestration, auditability, and release management for production agents.
actionable_takeaway: Add context, governance, and release checks to any recurring Codex automation before scaling it.

title: Kiro and the AI IDE Market
author: Stephen O'Grady / RedMonk
why_it_matters: The analysis frames remote cloud sessions and the model-versus-harness question across AI coding tools.
actionable_takeaway: Track whether personal workflow should standardize on model, app harness, or repository conventions.

title: obsidian-second-brain
author: Eugeniu Ghelbur
why_it_matters: The repo packages Obsidian as an AI-first second brain across Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini, OpenCode, Hermes, and Pi.
actionable_takeaway: Normalize AI Radar knowledge cards so multiple coding agents can read and reuse them.

title: How to Build an AI Second Brain with Claude Code and Obsidian
author: MindStudio Team
why_it_matters: The guide explains the shift from passive notes to a second brain that reads context and automates repeated tasks.
actionable_takeaway: Use structured note fields instead of freeform summaries when building agent-readable memory.

title: OpenAI Status incident on API, ChatGPT, and Codex
author: OpenAI Status
why_it_matters: Daily automation depends on external services; failures should be recorded instead of silently retried destructively.
actionable_takeaway: Add dependency health checks and failure reporting to the AI Radar runbook.
